codeReview.summary: Reviewed the prior failure report, mission metadata, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit a3e92f5fb60151b6a527739b77e6d666d18e8020. The fix removes the fixed post-exit timeout in `run_proxy_with_io()`, stops only producers that could enqueue new child input, drops the final child-input sender, and then joins the child stdin writer plus stdout/stderr forwarding tasks so draining continues until EOF. The new regression test uses a descendant fixture that keeps inherited stdout/stderr open after the parent exits and verifies the wrapper preserves the full delayed trailing payload beyond the old 3-second cutoff. I found no remaining blocking issues in this fix.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fix feature now explicitly requires draining wrapper stdout/stderr until EOF after child exit, but the validation contract still says the wrapper must always exit within ≤5 seconds of child exit. Those rules conflict in descendant-output cases, so future workers or reviewers could validate the wrong behavior unless the shared contract is clarified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:558-565 requires removing the fixed timeout and draining to EOF; validation-contract.md:38-39 still states that after the codex child exits the wrapper must exit within ≤5 seconds.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the prior failure, transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit a3e92f5fb60151b6a527739b77e6d666d18e8020. This rerun adequately fixes the original drain-timeout failure by removing the 3-second race and adding regression coverage for delayed trailing stdout/stderr after child exit. No blocking issues found.
